violations.description: Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Different species of raw animal food was not properly separated from each other or arranged according to minimum cooking temperatures. Raw fish being stored below raw ground meats and raw beef in the walk-in cooler.--Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. Raw pork stored above ready to eat foods in the walk-in cooler.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. Reach in cooler at the north end of the cook-line had potentially hazardous products temping between 45-50 degrees.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Inside of the microwaves on the cook line need to be cleaned. The drawers on the cook line have food debris build-up and need to be cleaned.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Underneath and behind the flattop has grease and food debris build-up and needs to be cleaned.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in the proper location to protect from contamination. Dirty rubber spatulas and utensils were being stored in a drawer with clean utensils.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors throughout the facility need to be cleaned.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. Wet mop being stored on the floor in the back was not placed in a position to dry properly.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Boxes of food were on the floor in the walk-in freezer.

Contaminated food disposition. — --Potentially hazardous food held for greater than four hours between 41F and 140F was not discarded when conditions require. Containers of sausage were left on the prep table and were not put back in the cooler when necessary.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Food contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in good repair or sound condition. Spatulas in the drawer, and food containers in the walk-in cooler are not in good repair.--Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. Gaskets for the drawers on the cook like are torn.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Gaskets on the cook line need to be cleaned. Underneath the cooking equipment needs to be cleaned, under the flattops.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Single use cups were on the floor in the dry storage area. To-go containers on the south microwave on the server line need to be inverted.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. The walk-in floors for both the cooler and the freezer need to be cleaned.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Method, clean, temp, concentration, exposure time, equipment/utensils sanitized (4 pts deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
